RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'s Amended Motion To Compel Initial Responses To Set One Special Interrogatories And To Impose Monetary Sanctions Against Cross-Complainant is GRANTED, in part. Pro Pavers shall provide verified Code-compliant responses to special interrogatories 1-34 within 20 days. The request for sanctions is denied.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Continued To Jan-10-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andre J. LeLievre, Esq. Tel. 916.929.0130 ext.251, for plaintiff/cross defendant, by Court Call;
Ian Bennett-Goldberg, Esq. of Weston Law Group, PC, Tel. 925.362.3120, for defendant/cross complainant.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling, with modification as reflected on the signed order. CROSS DEFENDANT NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT entered against it on October 13, 2017 is granted. NCCS has amply showed grounds to set aside the default per CCP 473(b) and doing so comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, especially where there is no conceivable prejudice to cross-complainant Pro Pavers Corp. by the grant of the motion. The conduct of counsel for Pro Pavers in notifying NCCS that it had until October 20 to avoid a default and then, in the midst of settlement discussions, stating on October 6 that a default would be taken 2 court days later falls well below the standards of civility expected of all counsel and is, without more, sufficient to warrant the granting of this motion. NCCS had the right to rely on the commitment that its default would not be taken till October 20 and its failure to file a response to the cross-complaint before October 20 is, at a minimum, excusable neglect. In addition, the court ordered that cross defendant must respond by Dec. 5, 2017. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.
